expressions.english: to watch and learn; to learn by seeing others do something
expressions.notes: 보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read

expressions.english: to learn to drink; to learn drinking manners
to learn to drive
to learn by oneself
to learn Korean
to learn in school
Where did you learn Korean?
I learned my drinking manners from my father.
Where did you learn such an expression?
expressions.notes:
그런 = (something) like that; such + noun
말 = language; what one says; expression; word; term

expressions.english: He learns everything fast.
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
사람 = person, people
뭐든지 = whatever it is, anything
빨리 = the adverb form of 빠르다.
-살 = age, years old
때 = time
처음 = first; for the first time

expressions.english: I learned how to drive for the first time when I was 20 years old.
I'm sure I learned about it (before), but I can't remember.
expressions.notes:
분명히 = for sure, certainly, without doubt
기억 = memory
안 = not
기억이 나다 = to remember

expressions.english: I am thinking of learning to play the guitar.
expressions.notes:
기타 = guitar
-어/여 보다 = to try + V-ing
생각 = thought; opinion; idea
-(으)ㄹ고 생각 중이다 = to be thinking of + V-ing

expressions.english: When I was young, I learned piano and ballet.
expressions.notes:
어릴다 = to be young
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
피아노 = piano
발레 = ballet

expressions.korean: 어제 배운 것을 벌써 다 잊어 버렸어요.
expressions.english: I've already forgotten what I learned yesterday.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
벌써 = already
다 = all; every (thing)
잊어버리다 = to forget

expressions.korean: 배우고 싶은 것은 많은데 시간이 없어 요.
expressions.english: There are a lot of things I want to learn, but I have no time.
expressions.notes:
-고 싶다 = to want to + V
많다 = to be a lot
시간 = time; hour
없다 = to not have; to be not there; to not exist

expressions.korean: 영어는 배우면 배울수록 더 어려워지는 것 같아요.
expressions.english: The more English I learn, the more difficult it seems to become.
expressions.notes:
영어 = the English language
-(으)면 -(으)ㄴ수록 = the more..., the more...
더 = more
어렵다 = to be difficult
-어/여지다 = to become + adjective
-것 같다 = It seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.korean: 아이들은 어른들이 하는 행동을 금방 보고 배워요.
expressions.english: Kids watch what grown-ups do and learn really quickly.
expressions.notes:
아이 = child, kid; baby
-들 = is a suffix used to indicate plural.
어른 = adult, grown-up
하다 = to do
행동 = behavior
금방 = soon, shortly; quickly

expressions.korean: 영어를 배우고 싶어하는 사람들이 많아 지고 있어요.
expressions.english: There is an increasing number of people who want to learn English.

expressions.korean: 고등학교 때 스페인어를 배웠는데, 지금 은 하나도 기억이 안 나요.
expressions.english: I learned Spanish back in high school, but I don't remember anything now.
expressions.notes:
고등학교 = high school
스페인어 = the Spanish language
지금 = now
하나도 = not even one; not at all
